---
title: "hycom_particle_tracker"
format: html
---

```{r}
## function that reads netcdf files and puts them in a workable stack object:
read_mover <- function(ncdfile, xvar = "u", yvar = "v", hycom_time = FALSE) {
  
  u <- terra::rast(ncdfile, subds = xvar)
  v <- terra::rast(ncdfile, subds = yvar)
  
  z_time <- terra::time(u)

  if (hycom_time) {
    z_time <- lubridate::as_date(
      as.numeric(str_extract(names(u), "MT=(.*)", 1)),
      origin = as_date("1900-12-31")
    )
  }
  
  return(list(u = u, v = v, z = z_time))
}

  # hy_date <-
    lubridate::as_date(
      as.numeric(str_extract(names(water_u)[1], "MT=(.*)", 1)),
      origin = as_date("1900-12-31")
    )

test <- 
hycom_path %>% 
  tibble(file = .) %>% 
  mutate(
    date = str_extract(file, "\\d{4}_\\d{2}_\\d{2}"),
    date = as_date(date)
  ) %T>%
  print()

temp <- 
  test %>% 
  filter(between(date, as_date("2024-01-01"), as_date("2024-02-01"))) %>% 
  slice(15:16) %>% 
  pull(file) %>% 
  read_mover(xvar = "u", yvar = "v", hycom_time = TRUE)


water_u    <- temp$u
water_u2    <- temp2$u
water_v    <- temp$v
water_v2    <- temp2$v
water_time <- temp$z
water_time2 <- temp2$z

comb_rast <- c(water_u, water_u2)

time(comb_rast) <- c(water_time, water_time2)
time(comb_rast)

plot(water_u)
plot(water_v)

```
